MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Jan-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT AMANDA YAN TING HUANG's (FROM CONSOLIDATED CASE CGC-21-595417) MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Cross-defendant Amanda Huang's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the cross-complaint filed by Desoto Ca Company and Yasir Khan is denied. None of the three claims in the cross-complaint are barred or invalid because of the plaintiff's' dismissal of claims against Huang.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must deliver it to Dept. 301.

Visiting Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HEK)

Motion To Contest David Davidson'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; Declaration Of Shivani Desai
Off Calendar Oct-22-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Nathan James, Esq. Seifer, lvjurken, et al. (415) 749 - 5900 njames@smdjlaw.com ATTORNEY FOR Plaintiff Amanda Huang
Brandon Jeremy Carr, Esq., The Law Offices of Arash Khorsandi, PC, 310-277-7529 B.Carr@arashlaw.com Attorney for Plaintiff Amanda Huang
David William Lerch, Esq., Cesari, Werner & Moriarty, 650-991-5126 dlerch@cwmlaw.com Attorney for Defendnat/Cross Complainant David Davidson
Robert Paul Wargo, Esq., Zelms Erlich Lenkov, 213-793-4356 rwargo@zellaw.com Attorney for Defendant/Cross Complainant Desoto Cab Company et al

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT YASIR KHAN, AND DESOTO CAB COMPANY, INC.'s Motion To Contest David Davidson's Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

The motion to contest defendant David Davidson's application for good faith settlement is denied. The court exercises its discretion to consider the untimely motion but concludes that moving parties Yasir Khan and Desoto Cab Company Inc. have not met their burden of showing that the settlement is not a good-faith settlement pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 877.6.

(SEE WORD MINUTES FOR ENTIRE RULING)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Off Calendar Nov-08-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion; And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Yasir Khan And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JEFFREY WOO, Esq. of Cesari, Werner and Moriarty (tel: 650-991-5126) for Defendant/Cross-Complainant David Davidson.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT DAVID DAVIDSON's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Yasir Khan And For Monetary Sanctions.

Matter not argued/contested. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

In view of this decidedly untidy record, and to promote the interests of justice and litigation expediency, the Court will issue an order compelling defendant Khan to appear for deposition on or before November 30, 2024, with the exact date to be determined and agreed to by the parties. The Court will also order that a completed substitution of attorney form detailing Khan's (and presumably Desoto's) representation shall be filed with the Court by November 15, 2024. That same decidedly untidy record does not support an award of sanctions to Davidson, and that request is denied. (See word minutes for complete ruling.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and email it directly to Judge Pro Tem Philip Ward at psw@hassard.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (525/JPT)
